name: Herman Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in half a cup of warm water. Allow it to stand for 5 minutes.
2. In a medium-sized nonmetal bowl, combine flour, salt, sugar, and two cups of warm water. Mix well until no dry ingredients remain.
3. Gradually stir in the remaining warm water with the dissolved yeast. Mix thoroughly.
4. Cover loosely and let the mixture rise at room temperature for 72 hours, stirring 2-3 times per day.
5. Transfer the fermented mixture to the refrigerator. Stir daily; use within 11 days.
6. To use: Remove the sourdough starter from the refrigerator and allow it to come to room temperature for at least one hour. Stir well and measure the amount needed for your recipe.
7. Replenish the remaining starter with Herman Food (1/2 cup sugar, 1 cup flour, 1 cup milk) and return to the refrigerator. Use within 2-14 days, stirring daily.
Yield: Approximately 2 cups of sourdough starter
Herman Food:
1/2 cup Sugar
1 cup Flour
1 cup Milk
